EF-1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ON MILLSTOWN RD AT 202 PM CST, 2.8 MILES SOUTHWEST OF PARK CITY. IT LIFTED AND DESCENDED SEVERAL TIMES BEFORE ENDING AT 206 PM CST, 1.8 MILES SOUTHEAST OF PARK CITY. THE TORNADO SEVERELY DAMAGED 8 BARNS, CAUSED MINOR ROOF DAMAGE TO A HOME, UPROOTED NUMEROUS TREES, AND SNAPPED TRUNKS OFF OF SEVERAL OTHER TREES. PATH LENGTH WAS 3 MILES. PATH WIDTH WAS 350 YARDS. MAX SPEED WAS 100 MPH.

NWS SURVEY TEAM THAT AN EF-1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF RICHARDSON ROAD AND ESTES DRIVE AROUND 1154 PM EST. THE TORNADO MOVED NORTHEAST INTO THE CITY OF BRANDENBURG CAUSING SIGNIFICANT TREE AND POWER LINE DAMAGE. THE MOST DAMAGE WAS NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF BROADWAY AND OLD STATE ROAD. SEVERAL BUILDING FAILURES WERE NOTED AS WELL AS MANY TWISTED TREES. THE TORNADO LIFTED NEAR THE OHIO RIVER AT 1205 AM. MAX WINDS IN THE BRANDENBURG AREA ALONG BROADWAY WERE AROUND 105 MPH.

NWS STORM SURVEY TEAM FOUND EF-1 DAMAGE IN THE CITY OF BRANDENBURG. TORNADO TOUCHED NEAR RICHARDSON ROAD AND ESTES DRIVE. THE TORNADO THEN MOVED NORTHEAST INTO DOWNTOWN BRANDENBURG CAUSING SIGNIFICANT TREE AND POWERLINE DAMAGE ALONG OLD STATE ROAD. STRUCTURAL DAMAGE WAS NOTED NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF OLD STATE ROAD AND BROADWAY. WINDS IN THIS AREA WERE ESTIMATED TO BE NEAR 105 MPH. THE TORNADO LIFTED NEAR THE OHIO RIVER AT 1205 AM EST.
POWER LINES DOWNED.
AT TERRY AND GREENWOOD ROAD. SPOTTER REPORTED DEBRIS HITTING THE SIDE OF THE HOUSE.

TREES DOWN ON ROADS MAINLY TO THE EAST OF HIGHWAY 55. US HIGHWAY 60 WAS CLOSED EAST OF SHELBYVILLE DUE TO SNAPPED POWER POLES.

NWS DAMAGE SURVEY TEAM FOUND AN EF-2 TORNADO TOUCHDOWN NEAR CEILIA IN CENTRAL HARDIN COUNTY. TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR HANSBOROUGH ROAD AND MOVED NORTHEAST TOWARD CECILIA. EF-2 DAMAGE WAS OBSERVED NEAR BETHLEHEM ACADEMY ROAD AND SOUTH SUNSET AVENUE WHERE SEVERAL HOMES SUSTAINED DAMAGE. ONE HOME COMPLETELY LOST ITS ROOF. TORANDO THEN MOVED NORTHEAST CAUSING DAMAGE AT CENTRAL HARDIN HIGH SCHOOL WHERE SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE WAS OBSERVED AT THE SCHOOLS SPORTS COMPLEX. THE SCHOOL SUFFERED ROOF DAMAGE.

*** 1 INJ *** INITIAL TOUCHDOWN OF EF-1 TORNADO. THE TORNADO DESTROYED SEVERAL BARNS AND ONE MOBILE HOME IN A 1/2 MILE LONG PATH BEFORE LIFTING. IT TOUCHED DOWN A SECOND TIME FOR A NEAR U.S. HIGHWAY 31W WHERE IT DESTOYED ONE MOBILE HOME AND SEVERAL BARNS AS WELL AS HEAVILY DAMAGING SEVERAL OTHER STRUCTURES. THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN BRIEFLY AT TWO OTHER LOCATIONS...DAMAGING BARNS AND HOMES ALONG BACON CREEK ROAD AND U.S. HIGHWAY 31. ONE RESIDENT COMPLAINED OF A SORE BACK BUT SOUGHT NO MEDICAL ATTENTION.
INITIAL TOUCHDOWN OF EF-1 TORNADO

POSSIBLE TORNADO. THE ROOF WAS BLOWN OFF CENTRAL HARDIN HIGH SCHOOL AT HIGHWAY 62 AND RING ROAD.

ON NELSONVILLE LOOP...A LARGE GRAIN SILO WAS DESTORYED. A SECOND TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN AT HIDDEN HOLLOW CONSTRUCTION COMPANY ON DOUBLE SPRINGS RD. THE TORNADO DESTORYED THE COMPANYS WORKSHOP AND FLIPPED OVER A TRAILER WITH FOUR OCCUPANTS INSIDE.

NWS DAMAGE SURVEY TEAM FOUND A SECOND TORNADO OF EF-2 STRENGTH TOUCHDOWN ABOUT 8 MILES EAST-NORTHEAST OF SPRINGFIELD AT APPROXIMATELY 106 AM EST. THIS TORNADO CAUSED EXTENSIVE DAMAGE TO A WELL-CONSTRUCTED OUT BUILDING. WOOD POSTS WERE SNAPPED AND PILED UP AND METAL SHEETING WAS BLOWN ABOUT A QUARTER MILE AWAY. TWO HUNDRED POUND CONCRETE JOINTS WERE DISPLACED. THE TORNADO LIFTED AT 108 AM EST ALONG ITS ONE MILE PATH. MAX WIND SPEEDS AND PATH WIDTH WERE 115 MPH AND 250 YARDS RESPECTIVELY.

NWS SURVEY TEAM FOUND DAMAGE CONSISTENT AT THE EF-1 LEVEL. WIND DAMAGE FIRST OCCURRED WITH DOWNBURST WINDS OF 70 MPH WEST OF STADMANTOWN LANE. EVIDENCE OF AN EF-0 TOUCHDOWN WAST NOTED ACROSS ELKHORN CREEK WHERE HARDWOOD TREES SHOWED A 70 YARD WIDE AND 1/8 MILE LONG PATH OF A STEEP RIDGE. THE TORNADO CONTINUED OVER THE RIDGE WHERE IT INCREASED TO EF-1 INTENSITY WITH WINDS OF 100 MPH. TWO HOMES ON THE WEST SIDE OF LUCAS LANE SUSTAINED EXTENSIVE ROOF DAMAGE...ALONG WITH 5 BARNS.

NWS SURVEY TEAM FOUND DAMAGE CONSISTENT AT THE EF-1 LEVEL. WIND DAMAGE FIRST OCCURRED WITH DOWNBURST WINDS OF 70 MPH WEST OF STADMANTOWN LANE. THE TORNADO CONTINUED OVER THE RIDGE WHERE IT INCREASED TO EF-1 INTENSITY WITH WINDS OF 100 MPH. TWO HOMES ON THE WEST SIDE OF LUCAS LANE SUSTAINED EXTENSIVE ROOF DAMAGE...ALONG WITH 5 BARNS.

AN NWS STORM SURVEY TEAM FOUND EXTENSIVE STRAIGHT-LINE WIND DAMAGE IN AND AROUND HARRODSBURG. MUCH STRUCTURAL DAMAGE OCCURRED AT THE COUNTY FAIRGROUNDS AND AT THE JAMES B HAGGIN MEMORIAL HOSPITAL. ALSO A TWO-STORY APARTMENT BUILDING HAD PART OF ITS WALL BLOWN OUT WHERE MAX WIND SPEEDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 80-90 MPH.

NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND AN EF-1 TORNADO TOUCH DOWN ONE QUARTER MILE EAST OF HARRODSBURG AT ABOUT 120 AM EST WHERE EXTENSIVE DAMAGE TO A WAREHOUSE AND TREES WAS FOUND. A TREE BRANCH WAS DRIVEN INTO THE SIDE OF A CAR AND A CHIMNEY WAS KNOCKED DOWN FROM A HOUSE. A FACTORY SUFFERRED MUCH ROOF DAMAGE AS WELL. THE TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND FOR ABOUT A MILE BEFORE LIFTING AT APPROXIMATELY 122 AM EST. MAX WIND SPEED WAS 105 MPH AND PATH WIDTH WAS ABOUT 300 FEET.

NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND AN EF-1 TORNADO TOUCH DOWN ONE QUARTER MILE EAST OF HARRODSBURG AT ABOUT 120 AM EST WHERE EXTENSIVE DAMAGE TO A WAREHOUSE AND TREES WAS FOUND. A TREE BRANCH WAS DRIVEN INTO THE SIDE OF A CAR AND A CHIMNEY WAS KNOCKED DOWN FROM A HOUSE. A FACTORY SUFFERRED MUCH ROOF DAMAGE AS WELL. THE TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND FOR ABOUT A MILE BEFORE LIFTING AT APPROXIMATELY 122 AM EST. MAX WIND SPEED WAS 105 MPH AND PATH WIDTH WAS ABOUT 300 FEET.
BARN AND ROOF DAMAGE WAS NOTED ALONG WITH SEVERAL TREES DOWN IN THE VICINITY OF ROGERS GAP ROAD EAST OF INTERSTATE 75.

NWS STORM SURVEY TEAM FOUND EXTENSIVE STRAIGHT-LINE WIND DAMAGE FROM THE SOUTHWEST PORTION OF JESSAMINE COUNTY NORTHEASTWARD TO 4 MILES SOUTH OF NICHOLASVILLE ROUGHLY BETWEEN 125 AM EST AND 135 AM EST. THE WORST DAMAGE OCCURRED 4 MILES SOUTH OF NICHOLASVILLE WHERE WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 80-90 MPH. A ONE-STORY HOUSE HAD THE BACK ROOF BLOWN OFF AND THROWN 100 YARDS. THIS HOUSE WAS ALSO PUSHED 1 FOOT OFF OF ITS FOUNDATION. A SMALL SHED WAS ALSO DESTROYED AND BLOWN 200 YARDS.
SEVERAL BARNS DAMAGED AND TREES DOWN
SEVERAL BARNS AND ONE HOME DESTROYED IN AND SURROUNDING CYNTHIANA. MANY MORE DAMAGED. NUMEROUS ROADS BLOCKED DUE TO POWERLINES AND TREES DOWNED. TRAILER FLIPPED IN WAL-MART PARKING LOT.
CORRECTED TIME...ROOF OFF A BUILDING NEAR NEW CIRCLE ROAD.
CORRECTED TIME...TREES DOWN...BARN DAMAGED...AND FENCES DOWN NEWTOWN PIKE NEAR IRON WORKS.

AN NWS STORM SURVEY TEAM FOUND STRAIGHT-LINE WIND DAMAGE ACROSS URBAN LEXINGTON. THIS OCCURRED ROUGHLY BETWEEN 129 AM EST AND 139 AM EST. MOST DAMAGE WAS FROM 50-60 MPH WITH TWO CONCENTRATED AREAS JUST WEST AND JUST EAST OF DOWNTOWN WITH MAX WIND SPEEDS OF 75 MPH. EXTENSIVE POWERLINE AND TREE DAMAGE OCCURRED WEST OF TOWN WHERE A HOUSE WAS DESTROYED FROM AN UPROOTED TREE. FURTHER EAST AMPLE DAMAGE OCCURED TO A TIRE STORE AND A HARDEES AND THE BLUEGRASS ANTIQUE MALL. TRAILERS WERE ALSO FLIPPED.
A BARN WAS DESTROYED.

*** 1 INJ *** NWS SURVEY TEAM FOUND EF-2 TORNADO DAMAGE JUST SOUTHWEST OF CYNTHIANA. THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR MT VERNON ROAD WITH WINDS ESTIMATED NEAR 100 MPH. DAMAGE CONTINUED ALONG CONNERSVILLE ROAD WHERE SEVERAL HOMES WERE DAMAGED. SEVERAL BARNS AND OUTBUILDINGS EXHIBITED DAMAGE CONSISTENT WITH EF-2 DAMAGE WITH WINDS AROUND 120 MPH. THE TORNADO CONTINUED NORTHEAST CAUSING EF-0 TREE AND BARN DAMAGE BEFORE LIFTING 1/2 MILE SOUTHWEST OF CYNTHIANA.

*** 1 INJ *** NWS SURVEY TEAM FOUND EF-2 TORNADO DAMAGE JUST SOUTHWEST OF CYNTHIANA. THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR MT VERNON ROAD WITH WINDS ESTIMATED NEAR 100 MPH. DAMAGE CONTINUED ALONG CONNERSVILLE ROAD WHERE SEVERAL HOMES WERE DAMAGED. SEVERAL BARNS AND OUTBUILDINGS EXHIBITED DAMAGE CONSISTENT WITH EF-2 DAMAGE WITH WINDS AROUND 120 MPH. THE TORNADO CONTINUED NORTHEAST CAUSING EF-0 TREE AND BARN DAMAGE BEFORE LIFTING 1/2 MILE SOUTHWEST OF CYNTHIANA.
BARNS WERE DAMAGED. A MAINTENENCE BUILDING WAS FLATTENED.

SEVERAL LARGE PINE TREES SNAPPED ON A GOLF COURSE. DAMAGE OCCURRED IN CONJUNCTION WITH ANTICYCLONIC CIRCULATION ON THE SOUTH SIDE OF A BOWING THUNDERSTORM SEGMENT.
NUMEROUS REPORTS OF ROOF DAMAGE...DAMAGE TO SHEDS AND VEHICLES...POWER LINES AND TREES DOWNED. MOST DAMAGE OCCURRED BETWEEN 145 AM AND 150 AM EST.

ONE LARGE BARN MOVED OFF ITS FOUNDATION...ANOTHER DESTROYED ALONG WITH PARTIAL DESTRUCTION OF A THIRD. DAMAGE OCCURRED IN ASSOCIATION WITH AN ANTICYCLONIC CIRCULATION ON SOUTH SIDE OF A BOWING THUNDERSTORM SEGMENT.
TREES WERE DOWNED AND SHINGLES WERE BLOWN OFF ROOFS ALONG ARNOLD HALE ROAD.

NWS STORM SURVEY TEAM FOUND EF-0 TORNADO DAMAGE ABOUT 4 MILES NORTHEAST OF WINCHESTER. TOUCHDOWN OCCURRED BETWEEN GAY EVANS AND PRETTY RUN ROADS. TWO BARNS WERE DESTORYED WHILE THREE BARNS HAD EXTENSIVE DAMAGE. ONE HOME SUFFERED STRUCTURAL DAMAGE AND MANY TREES WERE UPROOTED AND SNAPPED. WINDS WITH THIS TORNADO WERE ESTIMATED TO BE BETWEEN 80 AND 85 MPH. PATH LENGTH WAS 3/4 OF A MILE WITH A WIDTH OF 125 YARDS.

NWS SURVEY TEAM FOUND DAMAGE CONSISTENT WITH A EF-0 LEVEL. THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ABOUT FOUR MILES NORTHEAST OF WINCHESTER BETWEEN GAY EVANS ROAD AND PRETTY RUN ROAD. TWO BARNS WERE DESTROYED...3 BARNS HAD SUBSTANTIAL DAMAGE AND A CORN CRIB WAS DESTROYED. ONE HOUSE HAD STRUCTURAL DAMAGE AND MANY TREES WERE UPROOTED AND A FEW SNAPPED.
SEVERAL BARNS DAMAGED AND SIX CATTLE KILLED.
STORAGE SHED BLOWN ONTO ROAD AT INTERSECTION OF STATE HIGHWAY 627 AND LOST FORK ROAD. LOCAL STORES ICE MAKING MACHINE OVERTURNED.
1 TRAILER DESTROYED BY FALLEN TREE ON PIKE ROAD. POWER LINES DOWN ON PYLES ROAD WITH POWER OUT IN AGIN SUBDIVISION. TREES DOWN COUNTYWIDE.
PART OF THE STRUCTURE OF A GAS STATION WAS BLOWN ON TO HIGHWAY 627 IN MADISON COUNTY...JUST SOUTHWEST OF THE FORT BOONESBORO STATE PARK.
TREES WERE DOWN ON SIMPSON LANE SOUTHWEST OF FORT BOONESBORO. TREES WERE ALSO BLOCKING HIGHWAY 388.

*** 4 FATAL, 4 INJ *** THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN 1/2 MILE NW OF HAYESVILLE, ABOUT 1/4 MILE N OF THE STATE LINE. IT MOVED NE AT 55 MPH, WIDENING TO ONE HALF MILE WIDE BY THE TIME IT HIT TRACY ROAD, WHERE IT DESTROYED 5 MOBILE HOMES, 3 SITE-BUILT HOMES, AND NUMEROUS OUTBUILDINGS AT 145 AM CST. 4 RESIDENTS WERE KILLED IN 3 SEPARATE MOBILE HOMES IN THIS AREA AND 4 OTHERS WERE INJURED. THE STORM CONTINUED NE INTO MONROE COUNTY WHERE IT WEAKENED AND BECAME INTERMITTENT IN ITS DAMAGE PATH BEFORE DISSIPATING IN METCALF CO.
